scope.summary: Driver Live nightly has reached the required three scheduled green runs, and the server CI-lane tile now embeds the current 22-lane scheduled/advisory taxonomy instead of a stale 19-lane copy.
out_of_scope: Weakening, narrowing, or marking ignored any GitHub Actions lane or Rust test.
Claiming the server required CI lane is green; latest heartbeat evidence still reports required:ci.yml red on older hosted SHA 3e87e88.
Changing the sibling rust-oracledb repository; this close consumes its hosted Live nightly evidence only.

entry_points.name: hosted driver Live nightly streak
entry_points.trace: `gh run list --repo MuhDur/rust-oracledb --workflow=live.yml --limit 20 --json databaseId,displayTitle,headSha,headBranch,status,conclusion,event,createdAt,updatedAt,url` showed the latest three scheduled Live runs all completed with conclusion=success: 29722438114 (2026-07-20T06:42:32Z, sha 4fc6aa222c25b6c9ac906b3b3d298b5b47d8f836), 29806957606 (2026-07-21T06:25:10Z, sha 537373a1add992ea5e4369dce30d00803efab2ed), and 29896713651 (2026-07-22T06:25:25Z, sha 3934a23054c6e861096c79c21734620978900989).

entry_points.name: hosted driver Live jobs are real completed live-test jobs
entry_points.trace: `gh run view --repo MuhDur/rust-oracledb 29722438114 29806957606 29896713651 --json jobs,...` was run one id at a time; each run contained `driver live tests (Oracle Free 23ai)` with status=completed and conclusion=success, including steps `Start Oracle Free 23ai`, `Wait for database ready`, `Bootstrap test schema`, `Verify connect-trace credentials are distinguishable`, and `Driver live tests (serial, ignored)`.

entry_points.name: server heartbeat observes the driver Live lane as green
entry_points.trace: `gh run view 29917633699 --log-failed` and the downloaded `ci-heartbeat-snapshot` show `MuhDur/rust-oracledb` lane `driver_scheduled:live.yml` at run 29896713651 with state=success, conclusion=success, head_sha=3934a23054c6e861096c79c21734620978900989, updated_at=2026-07-22T06:37:16Z. The same heartbeat remains red for server `required:ci.yml`; that is reported here as out-of-scope, not hidden.

entry_points.name: CI-lane tile taxonomy sync
entry_points.trace: `python3 - <<'PY' ... parse docs/ci_taxonomy.json and crates/oraclemcp-core/ci_taxonomy.json ... PY` showed the repo-root taxonomy had 22 watched scheduled/advisory lanes while the crate-local embedded copy had 19. Commit 3c524c55 copies the generated root taxonomy into `crates/oraclemcp-core/ci_taxonomy.json`, restoring byte identity and preventing the dashboard tile from silently omitting current watched lanes.

entry_points.name: scoped CI-lane tile tests
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core ci_lane -- --nocapture` failed before the fix at `operator_ci_lanes_route_serves_a_fresh_configured_snapshot` with lanes length 19 vs catalog length 22, then passed after 3c524c55 with 9 passed, 0 failed.

entry_points.name: heartbeat and taxonomy guard checks
entry_points.trace: `bash scripts/test_ci_heartbeat.sh` passed (`ci-heartbeat: advisory lane honesty regression OK ...`), and `python3 scripts/ci_taxonomy.py --check` exited 0 after the embedded taxonomy sync.

limitations: The three-green streak is hosted elapsed evidence from the sibling driver repository. This oraclemcp commit fixes the server evidence/tile copy that had gone stale; it does not modify driver code.

The latest inspected server CI Heartbeat run 29917633699 still exits 1 because `required:ci.yml` is red on hosted SHA 3e87e88 and scheduled server mutation/loom evidence is red/unknown. Those are separate lanes, not hidden by this close.
The local shared checkout still contains unrelated untracked files outside this scope.
